'''Francis Meli''' (born 27 April 1979) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s. A [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] and [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] international representative centre or winger, he played in the [[National Rugby League]] for New Zealand club, [[Auckland Warriors]] before playing in the [[Super League]] for English clubs, [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] and [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Born in Apia, Samoa on 27 April 1979, Meli grew up in the Auckland suburb of [[Titirangi]],<ref>[http://localhistoryonline.shorelibraries.govt.nz/cgi-bin/PUI?e=-----------1-0-0---------1-0-0-&a=d&c=supercol&cl=CL4.M.Meli,%20Francis&d=wanw-WNI-AAA-2452 Meli gets ready to sock it to them] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110724195539/http://localhistoryonline.shorelibraries.govt.nz/cgi-bin/PUI?e=-----------1-0-0---------1-0-0-&a=d&c=supercol&cl=CL4.M.Meli,%20Francis&d=wanw-WNI-AAA-2452 |date=24 July 2011 }} ''Western Leader'', 13 March 2003</ref> Meli began his rugby league career at the age of 9 when he joined the Avondale Wolves.<ref name="TVNZ_501061">{{cite web|url=http://tvnz.co.nz/content/501061|title=Warriors profile: Francis Meli|date=14 April 2005|work=[[One Sport]]|access-date=30 September 2011}}</ref> He eventually joined City Marist and played for the [[Marist Saints]] in the [[Auckland Rugby League]].

==Club career==
===New Zealand Warriors===
Meli was then signed by the [[Auckland Warriors]], and made his [[NRL]] début against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] on 2 May 1999. Due to a feeder arrangement with the Warriors, Meli also played several games for [[Souths Logan Magpies|Brisbane Souths]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref>Richard Becht [http://www.scoop.co.nz/stories/SP0003/S00018.htm Backs To The Future] ''Scoop Sport'', 16 March 2000</ref>

He played for the Warriors on the wing in their [[2002 NRL Grand Final]] loss to the [[Sydney Roosters]]. In the post-season Meli was selected to go on the [[2002 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain and France]]. He was played on the wing in five [[Test match (rugby league)|test match]]es and scored 4 [[Try (rugby)|tries]].

Meli scored a club record 23 tries for the Warriors in the [[2003 NRL season]]. He scored 5 of those tries in a single finals game against the [[Canterbury Bulldogs]], a feat still unbeaten in the finals. He played his 50th match for the club on 6 April 2003 and his 100th match against the Wests Tigers on 9 April 2005.<ref>[http://localhistoryonline.shorelibraries.govt.nz/cgi-bin/PUI?e=-----------1-0-0---------1-0-0-&a=d&c=supercol&cl=CL4.M.Meli,%20Francis&d=wanw-WNI-AAA-2750 Francis Meli will play his 50th NRL match for the Warriors] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110724195530/http://localhistoryonline.shorelibraries.govt.nz/cgi-bin/PUI?e=-----------1-0-0---------1-0-0-&a=d&c=supercol&cl=CL4.M.Meli,%20Francis&d=wanw-WNI-AAA-2750 |date=24 July 2011 }} ''Western Leader'', 4 April 2003</ref><ref>[http://localhistoryonline.shorelibraries.govt.nz/cgi-bin/PUI?e=-----------1-0-0---------1-0-0-&a=d&c=supercol&cl=CL4.M.Meli,%20Francis&d=wanw-WNI-AAB-0452 Warrior makes it to 100] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110724195520/http://localhistoryonline.shorelibraries.govt.nz/cgi-bin/PUI?e=-----------1-0-0---------1-0-0-&a=d&c=supercol&cl=CL4.M.Meli,%20Francis&d=wanw-WNI-AAB-0452 |date=24 July 2011 }} ''Western Leader'', 27 May 2005. p.1.</ref> He was named the [[New Zealand Warriors 2003|2003 New Zealand Warriors’]] Player of the Year.

===St Helens===
At the start of 2006 Meli joined [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]. He played for St Helens on the wing in their [[2006 Challenge Cup]] Final victory against the [[Huddersfield Giants]]. 
[[File:Photo-0271.jpg|thumb|right|Meli warming up for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[2006 Super League Grand Final]] against [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Old Trafford]]]]
St Helens reached the [[2006 Super League Grand final]] to be contested against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], and Meli played on the wing, scoring a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in Saints' 26-4 victory.

As 2006 Super League champions, St Helens faced 2006 NRL Premiers the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[2007 World Club Challenge]]. Meli played on the wing in the Saints' 18-14 victory.

In his first three years at the club he acquired three Challenge Cup winning medals, a World Club Challenge winning medal and a Grand Final winning ring. He also played in 2008's [[Super League XIII#Grand Final|Super League XIII Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref name="2008 Grand Final">{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642170.stm|title=2008 Grand Final|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-04|access-date=2008-10-05}}</ref>

Visa issues due to the United Kingdom's new requirements curtailed the start of his 2009 campaign; however, these issues were suffered by many imports that season.<ref>Brad Walter and Maria Tsialis [http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/held-up-australian-players-in-limbo-after-changes-to-processing-ofvisa-applications-20091123-iwxv.html Held up: Australian players in limbo after changes to processing of visa applications] ''Sydney Morning Herald'', 6 February 2009</ref> He arrived back in the country five rounds late and was forced to start his season in the reserves.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2009/mar/18/francis-meli-st-helens-leeds|title = Rugby league: Francis Meli back at St Helens but not in contention to face Leeds Rhinos|website = [[TheGuardian.com]]|date = 18 March 2009}}</ref>

He played in the [[2009 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=AFP|title=Leeds makes it Super League hat-trick|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2009-10-11/leeds-makes-it-super-league-hat-trick/1098564|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=[[Australian Broadcasting Corporation|ABC]] News|date=11 October 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121113060836/http://www.abc.net.au/news/2009-10-11/leeds-makes-it-super-league-hat-trick/1098564 |archive-date=13 November 2012}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Fletcher|first=Paul|title=St Helens 10-18 Leeds Rhinos|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8290503.stm|access-date=22 November 2019|work=BBC Sport|date=10 October 2009}}</ref>

At the start of 2010 Meli was granted compassionate leave to briefly return to New Zealand.<ref>[https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/jan/22/francis-meli-returns-home-st-helens Francis Meli returns home as St Helens impress in testimonial] ''The Guardian'', 22 January 2010</ref><ref>[http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/10/01/22/RUGBYL_St_Helens.html Saints Clear Meli to Head Home] ''sportinglife.com''</ref>

He played in the [[2011 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-match-report?newsfeed=true|title=Leeds claim Grand Final glory as inspired Rob Burrow sinks St Helens|date=8 October 2011|work=Guardian|access-date=21 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8815944/St-Helens-16-Leeds-32-match-report.html|title=St Helens 16 Leeds 32|date=8 October 2011|work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=21 November 2019}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
Meli played for Super League side [[Salford Red Devils]] for the 2014 season after signing a one-year contract at the club. Meli retired at the conclusion of the 2014 season after failing to be offered a new contract.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/meli-wont-offered-new-salford-7649451|title = Meli won't be offered new Salford deal|date = 21 August 2014}}</ref>

[[File:Francis Meli 2008.jpg|thumb|right|Meli in action for Samoa in 2008]]

==International career==
===Samoa===
Meli played for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/World_Cup_2000/Game_2/Samoa-vs-New_Zealand_Maori.html|title=Samoa vs. New Zealand Maori|last=Shawn Dollin, Andrew Ferguson and Bill Bates|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=9 February 2012}}</ref>

===New Zealand===
In 2001 he then switched allegiance to [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]], playing in 14 test matches between 2001 and 2004.

Meli was selected to play on the wing for New Zealand in the [[2004 Anzac Test]] against Australia in [[Newcastle, New South Wales]], the Kiwis losing to the Kangaroos 10-37. At the end of the season he was also selected to play for New Zealand on the wing in all four of their matches at the [[2004 Rugby League Tri-Nations]].

===Samoan return===
In 2008 Meli played for [[Samoa national rugby league team#|Samoa]] in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]].<ref name="Samoa name World Cup Squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=16750|title=Samoa name World Cup Squad|publisher=League Unlimited|date=2008-08-05|access-date=2008-08-06|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110717060051/http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=16750|archive-date=17 July 2011|df=dmy}}</ref><ref name="Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads">{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7658506.stm|title=Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-08|access-date=2008-10-09}}</ref>

Meli was named in the Samoan squad for the [[2009 Pacific Cup]].<ref>[http://www.therhinos.co.uk/news/7999.php Poching names strong Samoan squad] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091010154654/http://www.therhinos.co.uk/news/7999.php |date=10 October 2009 }} ''therhinos.co.uk'', 6 October 2009</ref>

===Exiles===
Meli was selected to play for the [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]] by coach [[Brian McLennan]]. He started the game on the wing against [[England national rugby league team|England]], and scored a try in the 16-12 Exiles win. Francis was selected again to represent the [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]] by new coach [[Daniel Anderson (rugby league)|Daniel Anderson]], Meli started Game 1 on the wing and scored a try in the 18-10 loss to [[England national rugby league team|England]]. Meli also featured on the wing for Game 2 and was instrumental in helping the Exiles to a 32-20 win by scoring 2 tries and helping the Exiles retain the [[International Origin]] trophy.

== Post-playing career and personal life ==
In 2022, Meli was drafted in as a replacement for [[Shontayne Hape]] as a on-field player for ''Match Fit.'' He is also the mental health coach for the group as he is child case manager with [[New Zealand Police]] since retiring from the game. He has a wife and 3 girls, as of 2022, the oldest was 16  and the youngest 2.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Match Fit Season 3 Ep 4 {{!}} SPORT {{!}} ThreeNow |url=https://www.threenow.co.nz/shows/match-fit/season-3-ep-4/S2339-580/M71406-895 |access-date=2023-05-06 |website=www.threenow.co.nz}}</ref>
